# Handoff Document
*Generated by /handoff*

## What we're building
**EPHotspot** — a centralized, multi-tenant WiFi hotspot management platform built on MikroTik infrastructure. Operators subscribe monthly to deploy hotspot nodes. End users download an Android app, buy data via card or airtime, and roam across any EPHotspot hotspot with one account and one data balance that never expires.

## Decisions made

- **Architecture:** Central multi-tenant platform. One RADIUS server. All MikroTik nodes are enforcement points only — all business logic lives centrally
- **Business model:** Operators pay a monthly platform subscription. They keep 100% of data revenue from their users
- **Pricing:** Platform owner sets all data packages globally. No per-operator custom pricing
- **Auth:** Google, Facebook, Apple SSO + phone number OTP. One account works across all hotspot locations
- **Packages:** Data-based (MB/GB), not time-based. Unused data rolls over on every purchase — never expires
- **Payments:** Peach Payments (card + instant EFT) + Bango (carrier billing — charges airtime, works over GSM with zero internet)
- **Onboarding flow:** Open guest SSID → captive portal QR code → download app → register → auto-connect to secure SSID via `WifiNetworkSuggestion` API → small configurable free trial
- **Free trial:** Configurable per deployment by platform owner. Absorbed by operator on capped packages, free on unlimited
- **Disconnection:** Hard cut at zero data. Push notifications at 20% and 10% thresholds. No grace period
- **MikroTik integration:** FreeRADIUS for auth + accounting. MikroTik REST API for provisioning + monitoring
- **Operator onboarding:** Manual by platform owner for now. No self-service wizard in MVP
- **Stack:** Node.js + Express/Fastify, PostgreSQL + Prisma, FreeRADIUS, Next.js (admin), React Native (mobile), Socket.io, Firebase Cloud Messaging, Peach Payments SDK, Bango API
- **Platform:** Android first. iOS-ready architecture (no iOS-specific code in MVP)

2. Resolve open questions below (fill in .env values)
3. Tell Claude Code: *"Work through all open GitHub issues in order, starting with Issue 1. Use PRD.md and HANDOFF.md as your source of truth."*
4. Manually onboard first MikroTik node to FreeRADIUS once Issue 4 is complete
5. Configure Peach Payments and Bango accounts — add API keys to `.env`
6. Set up Firebase project — add FCM credentials to `.env`

## Open questions
- Node.js framework: Express or Fastify? (Fastify recommended for performance)
- OTP SMS provider: Africa's Talking or Twilio? (Africa's Talking is cheaper and SA-native)
- Hosting: where will the central RADIUS server and backend be deployed? (Suggest Hetzner or DigitalOcean)
- What is the monthly operator subscription price?
- What are the initial data package sizes and prices (ZAR)?
- What is the free trial size (MB)?
